The Prudential Insurance Company Of America?S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Expert Depositions, And To Reopen Discovery For Limited Purpose Of Completing Those Depositions
Off Calendar Feb-15-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT THE PRUDENTIAL INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA's Motion To Compel Expert Depositions, And To Reopen Discovery For Limited Purpose Of Completing Those Depositions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion DENIED. While I encourage the parties to meet-and-confer and agree to some sort of process for taking the expert witness depositions before or during trial, the facts remain as follows: Prudential noticed the depositions for dates to be completed before the close of expert discovery, Plaintiff made the expert witnesses available, Prudential canceled its own depositions, including saying it was being "crystal clear" that Prudential was "not proceeding with the depositions" while there were outstanding issues about which the parties were meeting and conferring, and Prudential said that it was Plaintiff's own "fault" for making the witnesses available at the times Prudential had selected. Weil Decl. Ex. L. Thus, Prudential created the situation from which it now seeks relief. This is inconsistent with a finding of good cause under CCP 2024.050.

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-17-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. TERENCE J. COLEMAN, Esq. of Pillsbury & Coleman, LLP (Tel: 415-433-8000) for plaintiff;
Atty. KRISTEN B. WEIL, Esq. of Dentons US LLP (Tel: 415-882-5000) for defendant the Prudential Insurance Company of America.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT THE PRUDENTIAL INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's (THE PRUDENTIAL INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA) motion for summary judgment is denied because there is a factual dispute as to plaintiff's entitlement to insurance benefits. Specifically, there exists a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff suffered from a "severe Cognitive Impairment which require[d] Substantial Supervision to protect [plaintiff] from threats to health or safety" when defendant denied plaintiff's benefits in October of 2019 and subsequently re-affirmed that denial. (UMF 2 [Smolenak, Ex. A]; Plaintiff's Additional Material Facts 18 [Dr. Schlatter Decl. ("She should not live alone.") (Coleman Decl., Exh. A at PRU-002968)], 69 [Pack Decl., par. 5 ("My evaluations on each occasion documented Mrs. Turley' s severe cognitive impairment resulting from the progression of Alzheimer's disease. My Reports document her cognitive impairment at a severe level.")], 144 [Dr. Nguyen letter (Coleman Decl., Exh. A at PRU-003409)]; see also UMF 7.) Defendant does not seek summary adjudication in the alternative so the court need not address defendant's other arguments regarding the viability of plaintiff's bad faith and exemplary damages claims.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)
